Requirement: The system shall register the app as a macOS login item so it launches automatically when the user logs in
The system shall present a confirmation dialog when the user attempts to quit while a session is active, informing them the current session will be marked incomplete
On launch, the system shall detect whether another instance is already running; if so, it shall bring the existing instance to the foreground and terminate the new one
When the app quits — whether user-initiated, OS-initiated, or via crash — the system shall write current state to persistent storage and mark any in-progress session as incomplete
On launch following a crash or unexpected quit, the app shall not attempt to resume the interrupted session, instead initialising from the last state recorded in Notion
The app shall launch automatically on macOS login
The app shall warn the user before quitting if a session is active
Only one instance of the app shall be permitted to run at any time
The app shall save state on quit (intentional or otherwise), with any in-progress session marked as incomplete
On launch following a crash or unexpected quit, the app shall recover to the last state saved to Notion rather than attempting to resume any interrupted session
The system shall display a cog icon in the popover that, when clicked, replaces the current popover view with the config screen
The config screen shall expose controls for: focus session duration, short break duration, long break duration, pomodoros per cycle, and Planning DB URL

When any config value is changed in the popover, the system shall persist the change locally immediately; the Notion config page shall then be updated via the API on a best-effort basis, with silent retry on failure
The app shall never read config from Notion at runtime; the Notion config page is write-only from the app's perspective, except on first launch or when no local config exists
On first launch, the system shall initialise config with default values (focus: 25 mins, short break: 5 mins, long break: 15 mins, pomodoros per cycle: 4) and write them to the Notion config page
Config values shall be subject to validation: session durations must be between 1 and 120 minutes; pomodoros per cycle must be between 1 and 8
The config screen shall include a back control that returns the user to the main popover view without requiring a full close and reopen
Config changes shall take effect from the next session; any session currently in progress shall not be affected by a config change
The config screen shall include an input field for a Discord webhook URL, clearly labelled, with an indication of whether it is currently set
The system shall validate that the Discord webhook URL field, if populated, conforms to the Discord webhook URL format; invalid URLs shall be rejected with a clear inline error
The Discord webhook URL shall be persisted locally and mirrored to the Notion config page alongside all other config values
The config screen shall include a field for the Planning DB URL, with a clear indication of whether it is currently set
The app shall provide a config screen accessible from a cog icon in the popover, which replaces the popover content when opened
The app shall maintain a config page in Notion that reflects the current app configuration
The popover config screen shall be the sole source of truth for configuration; the Notion config page is a read-only mirror
The user shall be able to configure focus session length, short break length, long break length, and the number of pomodoros per cycle
The app shall allow the user to configure a Discord webhook URL to enable phone notifications for session events
The app shall allow the user to configure the Planning DB URL in Notion

On each focus session start attempt, the system shall check whether a planning session has been completed since the last 4AM boundary; if not, it shall gate the user into a planning session before proceeding
The system shall persist the timestamp of the last completed planning session in electron-store, using a 4AM daily cutoff to determine whether planning is required
When a planning session begins, the system shall auto-create a row in the Planning DB with an auto-generated Name and today's date; if a row for today already exists it shall use the existing one
The planning session shall have no timer; the system shall record the start time when planning begins and the end time when the user clicks 'Planning Complete, Sync From Notion'

On clicking 'Planning Complete, Sync From Notion', the system shall: read the Focus Time Goal from today's Planning DB row; calculate Pomodoro Goal as ceil(Focus Time Goal / configured focus session duration); write the calculated Pomodoro Goal back to the Planning DB row; and refresh the task list from the Focus Tasks DB
After the sync, the system shall read the Tasks to Complete relation from today's Planning DB row and surface those tasks prominently in the popover task picker
If the Planning DB URL is not configured, the system shall display a clear error state in the popover directing the user to config, and shall not allow planning to be started or completed
If the user completes planning without setting a Focus Time Goal, the system shall allow completion and the popover shall omit goal indicators gracefully with no error
The planning session shall be logged to the Pomodoro Sessions DB with Type = Planning and actual elapsed duration, consistent with ST-PR-01
The app shall enforce a daily planning gate, requiring a completed planning session before any focus session can be started each day
The app shall auto-create a row in the Planning DB at the start of each planning session, keyed to today's date
The planning session shall be open-ended — the user ends it manually by clicking a 'Planning Complete, Sync From Notion' button
On completing planning, the app shall read goals and planned tasks from Notion and make them available in the popover for the rest of the day
The Planning DB URL must be configured before the planning feature is usable; the app shall block the user with a clear error directing them to config if it is not set
The system shall display the remaining session time (MM:SS) in the menu bar during any active session — Focus, Short Break, or Long Break
The system shall display a static icon in the menu bar when the app is in an Idle state, with no countdown visible
The system shall differentiate the six app states (Focus, Short Break, Long Break, Paused, Planning, Idle) in the menu bar icon using a combination of distinct symbol and colour
The system shall open the popover when the user clicks the menu bar icon, regardless of current app state
The menu bar icon shall have visually distinct appearances for each app state: Focus, Short Break, Long Break, Paused, Planning, and Idle
The menu bar icon shall display a live countdown when a session is active, and a static icon when idle
The menu bar icon shall follow native macOS UI conventions in its visual design
Clicking the menu bar icon shall open the app popover
The system shall create a relation property on the Focus Tasks DB linking each task to exactly one Project
The system shall create a relation property on the Pomodoro Sessions DB linking each session to exactly one Focus Task
The system shall expose rollup properties so that Actual Pomodoros on a task auto-calculates from its linked sessions, and Total Pomodoros on a project auto-calculates from its linked tasks
Projects DB — Name: Title field. The project name.
Projects DB — Status: Select field. Options: Active / On Hold / Completed / Archived
Projects DB — Area: Select field. No pre-populated options — user adds their own (e.g. Work, Personal, Learning, Health)
Projects DB — Color / Icon: Icon and Cover properties for visual scanning in dashboard views
Projects DB — Total Pomodoros: Rollup field. Count of completed focus sessions rolled up from Focus Tasks
Projects DB — Target Pomodoros: Number field. Optional effort goal for the project
Projects DB — Due Date: Date field. Optional project deadline
Projects DB — Notes: Rich text field. Freetext context for the project
Focus Tasks DB — Name: Title field. The task name.
Focus Tasks DB — Project: Relation to Projects DB. Required on every task.
Focus Tasks DB — Status: Select field. Options: Unscheduled / Scheduled / Done / Abandoned
Focus Tasks DB — Scheduled Date: Date field. Nullable — null means Backlog. Tasks where Scheduled Date is on or before today and not Done are surfaced as active in the app
Focus Tasks DB — Originally Scheduled: Date field. Set once when Scheduled Date is first assigned. Never updated on reschedule. Used to calculate days overdue.
Focus Tasks DB — Days Overdue: Formula field. Calculated as today() minus Originally Scheduled, where Scheduled Date is before today and Status is not Done
Focus Tasks DB — Priority: Select field. Options: P1 / P2 / P3
Focus Tasks DB — Estimate: Number field. User's expected pomodoro count for this task
Focus Tasks DB — Actual: Rollup field. Count of completed focus sessions linked to this task. Auto-calculated from Pomodoro Sessions DB.
Focus Tasks DB — Type: Select field. Options: Deep Work / Admin / Communication / Creative / Research
Focus Tasks DB — Due Date: Date field. Hard deadline if applicable. Separate from Scheduled Date.
Focus Tasks DB — Notes / Context: Rich text field. Where the user left off and what to pick up next session
Focus Tasks DB — Created: Created Time field. Auto-populated by Notion
Focus Tasks DB — Completed Date: Date field. Set automatically when Status transitions to Done
Each Focus Task row shall have a Notion button property labelled 'Reschedule to Today' that sets Scheduled Date to the current date
The 'Reschedule to Today' button shall not modify the Originally Scheduled field, preserving the original date for overdue tracking

The Focus Tasks DB header shall include a button labelled 'Reschedule All Overdue' that bulk-sets Scheduled Date to today for all tasks where Scheduled Date is before today and Status is not Done. No confirmation required.
Tasks with a Scheduled Date in the future shall be valid and stored, but shall not be surfaced in the app's task picker until their scheduled date arrives

Pomodoro Sessions DB — Name: Title field. Auto-generated by the app in the format '[logical-date] · [Type] · Session [N]', where N is the count of sessions of that type logged on the same logical day using the 4AM boundary (e.g. '2026-05-20 · Focus · Session 3' for the third focus session of that logical day).
Pomodoro Sessions DB — Task: Relation to Focus Tasks DB. Null for break sessions.
Pomodoro Sessions DB — Project: Rollup from Task relation. Allows filtering sessions by project without extra steps.

Pomodoro Sessions DB — Date: Date field. The logical date of the session, determined using the 4AM daily boundary: sessions occurring between midnight and 04:00 are attributed to the previous calendar date, consistent with the app's daily gate logic.
Pomodoro Sessions DB — Start Time: Date field with time. Session start timestamp.
Pomodoro Sessions DB — End Time: Date field with time. Session end timestamp.
Pomodoro Sessions DB — Duration (mins): Formula field. End Time minus Start Time in minutes.
Pomodoro Sessions DB — Type: Select field. Options: Focus / Short Break / Long Break / Planning
Pomodoro Sessions DB — Cycle Number: Number field. Which pomodoro in the current 4-cycle block (1-4). Null for breaks and planning sessions.

Pomodoro Sessions DB — Completed: Checkbox field. True if the timer ran to completion, or if the user ended the session early and chose Complete rather than Abandon; false if the session was abandoned.
Pomodoro Sessions DB — Interruptions: Number field. Count of interruptions logged during the session
The app shall run the planning session timer only — it shall not open Notion or any external app automatically when the session begins
The app shall write a session record to the Pomodoro Sessions DB with Type = Planning when a planning session ends
Planning sessions shall be excluded from all pomodoro count statistics on the dashboard
The task picker shall query Focus Tasks DB for records where: Scheduled Date is on or before today AND Status is not Done AND Status is not Abandoned
Tasks shall be ordered in the picker: overdue tasks first (oldest first), then today's tasks ordered by Priority (P1 → P3)
Overdue tasks shall display a label showing the number of days overdue (e.g. '3 days overdue'), derived from the Days Overdue formula field
The picker shall provide an option to browse the full backlog (Unscheduled tasks) for cases where the user wants to pull in additional work

When a focus session ends as Complete — whether the timer ran to zero or the user chose early complete — the app shall prompt the user to mark the task as Done or leave it Scheduled for a future session; no task status prompt shall appear when a session is Abandoned.
Daily Dashboard: show all tasks where Scheduled Date = today and Status = Scheduled
Daily Dashboard: show all tasks where Scheduled Date is before today and Status is not Done, with days overdue visible
Daily Dashboard: show count of focus pomodoros completed today (Sessions DB: Type = Focus, Completed = true, Date = today)

Daily Dashboard: show progress toward the two daily planning goals — (1) focus time accumulated today vs Focus Time Goal (in minutes), and (2) Tasks to Complete that are marked Done vs the total set for today; if no planning has been completed for the current day these indicators shall be omitted.
Daily Dashboard: show today's session log in chronological order
Weekly Dashboard: show total pomodoros per project for the current week
Weekly Dashboard: show tasks completed this week vs tasks added this week
Weekly Dashboard: show estimate accuracy — average difference between Estimate and Actual across tasks completed this week
Weekly Dashboard: show the user's current streak — consecutive logical days (as determined by the 4AM daily boundary) on which at least one focus session was completed.
Weekly Dashboard: show session completion rate — percentage of started focus sessions that ran to completion this week
The Focus Tasks DB shall include a rollup field counting the number of linked session records where Type = Focus and Completed = true, representing total completed focus sessions per task
The Focus Tasks DB shall include a rollup field summing the Duration (mins) of all linked session records where Type = Focus, representing total focus time spent on each task

The Focus Tasks DB shall include a rollup field summing total focus time in minutes across all linked sessions where Type = Focus, computed from the Duration field in the Sessions DB; actual elapsed duration is used in all cases, including sessions ended early by the user — an 8-minute early-complete counts as 8 minutes, not the configured session length.
When the user marks a task as complete from the popover, the app shall update the Status field of the corresponding Focus Tasks DB entry to Done via the Notion API
Planning DB — Name: Title field, auto-generated by the app in the format 'Planning — DD MMM YYYY'
Planning DB — Date: Date field, set by the app to today's date using the 4AM boundary, used as the key for querying today's row
Planning DB — Focus Time Goal: Number field in minutes, set by the user in Notion during the planning session
Planning DB — Pomodoro Goal: Number field, written back by the app after calculating ceil(Focus Time Goal / configured focus session duration)
Planning DB — Tasks to Complete: Relation field linking to Focus Tasks DB; the user populates this during planning to indicate which specific tasks they intend to complete today
The app shall use Notion as its persistent data store, organised across three linked databases: Projects, Focus Tasks, and Pomodoro Sessions
The three databases shall be related hierarchically: Projects → Focus Tasks → Pomodoro Sessions, so that data rolls up automatically from sessions to tasks to projects
The user shall never need to manually update the Pomodoro Sessions database — the app writes to it automatically

The Focus Tasks DB shall be the primary list of work the user intends to do, organised by project and schedulable to specific dates

Requirement: Tasks shall be schedulable to a specific date rather than a status bucket, so that unfinished tasks automatically become overdue without manual intervention
Functional Requirements: N-FR-14 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-14-366da49ee11281f9bef5fc3385a59667?pvs=21), N-FR-15 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-15-366da49ee112819d8489d1041cf11f9b?pvs=21), N-FR-16 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-16-366da49ee1128170a7e5e6f6c0b428db?pvs=21), N-FR-17 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-17-366da49ee112811c87cffaf75fc75a50?pvs=21), N-FR-26 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-26-366da49ee112814f8acacf9b360f08e9?pvs=21), N-FR-27 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-27-366da49ee112811cb6ccc487cd9e0c75?pvs=21), N-FR-28 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-28-366da49ee112819c8351f14100f6e69a?pvs=21), N-FR-29 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-29-366da49ee11281b098ffefa30fc5f881?pvs=21)

Requirement: The Focus Tasks DB shall track both the user's estimated and actual pomodoro count per task, enabling improvement of estimation accuracy over time
The Pomodoro Sessions DB shall act as an append-only event log, written to automatically by the app at the end of every session

Requirement: Session records shall contain enough data to power all dashboard statistics without requiring additional manual input from the user

Requirement: The app shall support a Planning Session mode — a distinct, time-boxed session for reviewing and scheduling tasks for the day — separate from the focus/break cycle
Planning sessions shall not count toward the user's daily pomodoro total, as they represent organisation time rather than focused work
When starting a pomodoro, the app shall present only actionable tasks — those scheduled for today or overdue — rather than the full backlog
Overdue tasks shall be visually distinguished from today's tasks in the picker, so the user can make an informed prioritisation decision
The Notion dashboard shall provide two views: a Daily view for in-session use and a Weekly/Project view for end-of-week review

Requirement: The dashboard shall surface statistics that support self-improvement, not just vanity counts
Functional Requirements: N-FR-53a (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-53a-366da49ee11281e7bd7ec7fb57ab7975?pvs=21), N-FR-55 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-55-366da49ee112812a9ec8f88c144880fb?pvs=21), N-FR-56 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-56-366da49ee1128167b990d99a8ed7dbfe?pvs=21), N-FR-57 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-57-366da49ee11281869969d3349cfee342?pvs=21), N-FR-58 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-58-366da49ee11281d9bf97fbbe4ebd5539?pvs=21), N-FR-59 (https://www.notion.so/N-FR-59-366da49ee11281b68450dd4216084f05?pvs=21)

Requirement: The Focus Tasks DB shall track the number of completed focus sessions and total focus time spent on each task, computed automatically from linked session records
The app shall be able to mark a Focus Task as complete in Notion from the popover, writing the status change via the Notion API
The app shall maintain a Planning DB in Notion with one row per planning day, used to store daily goals and link planned tasks
The system shall send a native macOS notification when any session timer completes (Focus, Short Break, or Long Break)
The system shall send a native macOS notification when any active session timer reaches 20% of its total duration remaining
Each notification shall include the session type and, for near-completion alerts, the time remaining
The system shall send a Discord webhook notification when a focus session completes, indicating the session is done and a break is starting
The system shall send a Discord webhook notification when a break timer reaches 20% of its total duration remaining, prompting the user to return to their desk
The system shall send a Discord webhook notification when a break session completes, indicating the break is over and it is time to focus
Discord webhook notifications shall only fire if a webhook URL has been configured in settings; if no URL is set the feature shall be dormant with no errors
Discord webhook calls shall be best-effort with silent retry on failure and shall never block or delay the app or its timer
Each Discord notification message shall be concise and scannable, including the event type and contextually relevant detail (e.g. time remaining for near-completion alerts, session type throughout)
The app shall use native macOS notifications to alert the user at key points during any session
Notifications shall include contextual information identifying the session type and remaining time where applicable
The app shall support Discord webhook notifications for key session events, enabling the user to receive alerts on their phone when away from their desk
The popover shall display two statistics: total pomodoros completed today and total focus time today (in minutes or hours)
The popover shall display a list of incomplete Focus Tasks from Notion with a Scheduled Date of today
If no tasks are scheduled for today, the task list shall display a default empty state message directing the user to add tasks via Notion and refresh
On popover open, the task list shall display the last cached result immediately, then trigger a background fetch and silently update the list on completion (stale-while-revalidate)
The popover shall include a manual refresh button that forces an immediate fetch of the task list from Notion
The start session button shall only be available when a task is selected and the app is in Idle or Break state

The popover shall display a cancel button during an active focus session; cancelling shall mark the session as incomplete in Notion, log a cancellation, and return the app to the start of the current pomodoro position
The popover shall display a pause/resume button during an active focus session; pausing shall halt the timer without advancing it

The popover shall display an 'End Early' button during an active focus session; triggering it shall prompt the user to mark the session as Complete or Abandon it. If Complete: the session shall count as a completed pomodoro, the cycle shall advance, and the user shall then be prompted whether the task itself is Done or should be rescheduled for a future session. If Abandoned: the session shall be logged with Completed = false and the app shall return to Idle without prompting for task status.
Upon completing a full focus session, the popover shall prompt the user to mark the current task as Done in Notion or leave it Scheduled for a future session.

The task selected at the start of a session shall persist as the pre-selected task for the next session in the cycle, provided it was not marked complete; if complete, the user shall be required to pick a new task
The user shall not be able to change the selected task while a focus session is active
The popover shall include a Plan My Day button accessible from the main popover view, initiating a Planning Session as defined in N-FR-41
The popover shall include a cog icon that opens the config screen as defined in CFG-FR-01
The popover shall display the Focus Time Goal and Pomodoro Goal from today's Planning DB row after planning is complete; if no goal was set these indicators shall be omitted
The popover shall display live progress toward the daily goals: pomodoros completed today vs Pomodoro Goal, and total focus time today vs Focus Time Goal
The popover shall display daily progress statistics for the current user
The popover shall present a task picker for selecting a Focus Task before starting a session
Task selection shall be mandatory before starting a focus session
The popover shall provide full session controls: start, cancel, pause/resume, and end early with task completion
The task list shall reflect only incomplete tasks from Notion scheduled for today, kept reasonably fresh without blocking the UI
The selected task shall persist across sessions within a cycle unless marked complete
The popover shall display the user's daily goals and progress toward them once planning is complete

On completion of a focus session, the system shall write a record to the Sessions DB with: auto-generated Name, Task relation linked to Focus Tasks DB, Date, Start Time, End Time, elapsed Duration, Type = Focus, Cycle Number, Completed = true
On cancellation of a focus session, the system shall write a session record with the same fields as ST-FR-01 but with Completed = false and actual elapsed duration at the point of cancellation
On early complete of a focus session, the system shall write a session record with Completed = true and actual elapsed duration at the point the user ended the session
On end of any break session — whether run to completion or cut short by the user starting a new focus session — the system shall write a record with the appropriate Type and actual elapsed duration
Every session record shall be written to electron-store immediately when the session ends, prior to any Notion API call being attempted
After writing locally, the system shall attempt to write the session record to Notion; on failure the record shall be marked pending_sync in electron-store and no data shall be lost
The system shall retry all pending_sync session records automatically every 5 minutes while the app is running, and on every app launch
If one or more session records are in a pending_sync state, the popover shall display a subtle sync status indicator so the user is aware data has not yet reached Notion
The app shall log a session record to the Pomodoro Sessions DB for every session that occurs — Focus, Short Break, Long Break, and Planning
All session records shall use actual elapsed duration rather than configured duration
Session data shall always be persisted locally via electron-store before any Notion write is attempted, ensuring no data is lost due to API failure
The app shall maintain a persistent sync queue so that any session record that fails to write to Notion is retried automatically until successful

The system shall track the current cycle position (1 through the configured pomodoros-per-cycle value) in local state, incrementing after each focus session that is completed or early-completed, and resetting to 1 after a long break session is logged
The app shall maintain an accurate cycle position throughout the focus/break cycle, resetting appropriately after each long break
The app shall require a valid Notion API integration token and the IDs of all required Notion databases to be configured before any functionality is available.
The Notion API integration token shall be stored using Electron's safeStorage API, which encrypts the value using the macOS Keychain; it shall never be written to disk in plaintext.

The config screen shall include an input field for the Notion API token (masked, with an indicator of whether one is currently stored) and URL fields for each of the four required Notion databases: Projects DB, Focus Tasks DB, Pomodoro Sessions DB, and Planning DB.

On first launch, when no valid API token is configured, the app shall display a blocking setup state in the popover preventing access to all timer and planning functionality, with a clear prompt directing the user to open the config screen and complete setup.

When a new or updated API token is saved in the config screen, the system shall validate it by making a lightweight test call to the Notion API; on success the token shall be persisted via safeStorage; on failure an inline error shall be shown and the previous token (if any) shall remain.

If a Notion API call fails with an authentication error at runtime, the app shall surface a non-blocking error banner in the popover directing the user to check their API token in config; this shall not suspend or affect the active timer.

The config screen shall include URL fields for the Projects DB, Focus Tasks DB, and Pomodoro Sessions DB, each with a clear label and an indication of whether the field is currently set; these are in addition to the Planning DB URL field (CFG-FR-12) and the Notion API token field (AUTH-FR-02).
On first launch, the system shall request macOS notification permissions via the standard system prompt.

If notification permissions are denied or revoked, all native macOS notifications shall be silently suppressed; the config screen shall display a persistent alert banner informing the user that notifications are disabled, with brief guidance to enable them in System Settings.
The notification permission status shall be checked on each app launch; the alert banner in the config screen shall appear or be dismissed automatically based on the current permission state.

Offline operation, local-only session fallback, and degraded-mode functionality are explicitly out of scope; the app requires an active internet connection and a reachable Notion API to function. API write failures while online are handled via the pending-sync queue (ST-FR-05–07).
During an active break session, the popover shall display a Cancel button; cancelling shall log the break record with Completed = false and actual elapsed duration, and transition the app to Idle.

During an active break session, the popover shall display a 'Skip Break' button; triggering it shall log the break record with Completed = true and actual elapsed duration, then transition the app to Idle. If no task is currently persisted (e.g. the previous task was marked complete), the Skip Break button shall be disabled.
The pause/resume control shall not be available during break sessions; breaks run uninterrupted until they complete, are cancelled, or are skipped.

The popover shall not display daily goal indicators or progress toward them until planning for the current day has been marked complete; prior to that, the goal section shall be absent from the popover entirely.

On completion or cancellation of a long break session, the app shall transition to Idle state with the cycle counter reset to 1 and the persisted task selection cleared; the user must select a task before the next focus session can begin.

The app shall reference all Focus Tasks and Notion database entries exclusively by their immutable Notion page ID; task property mutations (name, status, priority, notes, etc.) shall not affect in-progress session records or task associations.

Deletion of a Focus Task in Notion while it is the active or persisted task in the app is an explicitly unsupported edge case and requires no error handling; users are expected not to delete tasks that have active, pending, or in-progress sessions.
The app shall never set a task's Status field to Abandoned via the Notion API; the Abandoned status is exclusively applied by the user directly within Notion.

If the task list cache was last fetched before the current 4AM boundary, the system shall perform a blocking refresh from Notion before displaying the task list on the next popover open; the stale-while-revalidate behaviour (POP-FR-04) applies only within the same logical day.
